summary: Permuter-driven matching cleanup for Link Arrow item code plus small Yoshi special-move fixes. The PR converted several near-matches to exact matches, especially in src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c, where .data reached 100% and multiple Link Arrow behavior functions matched. It also used targeted inline helpers, stack-padding changes, type/prototype corrections, boolean cleanup, named math constants, and deliberate fake/assert helper handling to improve codegen without broad semantic rewrites.

key_files.role: Main Link Arrow decomp work; most of the diff and most reported matching gains, including .data, hit/shield, reflected, collision, and animation routines.
Header prototype/data declaration updates for fn_802A81C4 and it_803F6A78.
Permuter/codegen fixes for Yoshi Egg Roll/SpecialS animation and collision routines.
Small variable lifetime/name adjustment in ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C for better matching.

what_changed: Renamed Link Arrow anonymous data arrays from generic numbers/numbers_l to symbol-style it_803F6A78 and it_803F6A98, and exported it_803F6A78 in itlinkarrow.h.

Reworked fn_802A81C4 from void fn_802A81C4(HSD_GObj*) to s32 fn_802A81C4(Item_GObj*) in both source and header, using GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ and indexing through (f32*) &it_803F6A28 for the random angle table logic.
Added __FILE__ overrides around fake_HSD_JObjSetRotation/fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate so HSD_ASSERT line/file behavior can pretend to originate from jobj.h, then restored __FILE__ to itlinkarrow.c.
Simplified many unnecessary HSD_GObj casts in itlinkarrow.c, e.g. GET_ITEM(gobj), it_80275158(gobj, ...), Item_80268E5C(gobj, ...), it_8026B894(gobj, arg1).

Refactored it_802A850C to initialize Item*, HSD_JObj*, and attributes up front, introduce explicit stack padding, use ftLib_80086630(...)->mtx directly, and nest the fighter owner/null check inside a scoped block.

Split Link Arrow animation/collision/hit-shield logic into small inline helpers, including itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline2, itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Coll_inline, and itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield_inline.
Converted numerous integer 0/1 returns and comparisons to bool/true/false in Link Arrow routines, while preserving decomp-needed control flow such as the goto end in itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield.
Changed itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Anim to duplicate fn_802A81C4-like logic with a comment that it is the same code but could not be inlined.
Updated Link Arrow reflected logic to use M_PI and M_PI_2 instead of raw double literals like 3.141592653589793 and 1.5707963267948966.

In Yoshi SpecialS, introduced permuter-generated inline wrappers such as perm_vel_inline, perm_neg_inline, perm_mul_inline, and perm_vel_x_inline to steer codegen; the source comment explicitly says some are almost certainly fake.
Adjusted Yoshi SpecialS stack padding from PAD_STACK forms to explicit u8 pad arrays in some functions and changed several condition forms, unsigned literal suffixes, and float comparisons.
In Yoshi SpecialHi ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C, changed temp_f4 to mag and kept the magnitude variable live across scopes before applying getStickDirX.
smart_moves: Used very small fake inline helpers to perturb compiler output without obscuring the surrounding decompiled logic; this converted several Yoshi SpecialS collision routines from near-matches to 100% according to the decomp.dev report.
Preserved evidence of fake/permuter-only constructs with an explicit comment, making future cleanup easier when a cleaner match is found.
Matched Link Arrow .data by replacing generic arrays with address-style symbols and by relying on offset indexing from it_803F6A28, which is a practical tactic when adjacent data layout matters.
Wrapped fake HSD_JObj helper assertions with __FILE__ manipulation instead of changing global assert behavior, localizing the workaround to the code that needs jobj.h assert metadata.
Preferred typed Item_GObj usage and GET_JOBJ/GET_ITEM accessors to eliminate cast noise while still improving matching.
Introduced helper splits only where they affected codegen-sensitive regions, especially Link Arrow motion/collision and hit-shield logic.
decomp_lessons: For data matches, symbol placement and adjacent-array layout can matter as much as the literal values; address-style names such as it_803F6A78 may be preferable to descriptive temporary names until the data is understood.
When a function almost matches, try changing variable lifetime and scope before making larger semantic rewrites; the SpecialHi temp_f4-to-mag change is a small example.

Inline wrappers that simply return a field, negate a value, or multiply arguments can change load ordering/register allocation enough to close tiny gaps, but they should be marked as likely fake if they are permuter artifacts.
Explicit u8 pad arrays and PAD_STACK placement are useful stack-layout controls; changing padding size/type can be part of the matching surface.
Use project math constants like M_PI and M_PI_2 where they match emitted constants and improve readability.
Boolean spelling can affect matching and readability; this PR repeatedly replaced return 0/1 with false/true and comparisons with direct bool checks where matching allowed it.

A helper that should logically be shared may still need to be duplicated if the original binary inlined one call site but not another; the PR explicitly notes this for itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Anim versus fn_802A81C4.

Do not assume uninitialized branches should be made safe if matching requires otherwise; it_802A850C_inline removed a previous NULL return with a todo saying the real behavior should just return uninitialized.
naming_conventions: Address-like data symbols use the it_803F6Axx form, e.g. it_803F6A28, it_803F6A78, and it_803F6A98.
Temporary item pointers were standardized locally as ip in rewritten Link Arrow routines, replacing some item variables.
Permuter-only helpers were named with a perm_*_inline pattern, e.g. perm_vel_inline, perm_neg_inline, perm_mul_inline, perm_vel_x_inline.
Fake base-library helper shims were named fake_HSD_JObjSetRotation and fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate and guarded with comments indicating they should be removed once lbcoll is matched.
Unknown Link Arrow routines retain existing address/behavior placeholders such as fn_802A81C4, it_802A850C, itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Coll, and itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use fake inline field accessors like perm_vel_inline(fp) for fp->gr_vel and perm_vel_x_inline(fp) for fp->self_vel.x to affect reloads and comparison codegen.
Use fake arithmetic wrappers like perm_neg_inline(attributes->xAC) and perm_mul_inline(attributes->xD8, ABS(...)) to steer expression lowering.
Use assignment inside an expression, as in fp->mv.ys.specials.x1C = ABS(fp->gr_vel = fp->self_vel.x), to alter ordering and collapse a longer temporary block.

Manipulate stack layout with explicit u8 pad arrays and PAD_STACK calls in functions such as ftYs_SpecialAirSLanding_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Coll, it_802A850C, itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield, and itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Anim.
Use pointer arithmetic over adjacent data, e.g. temp_r3 = (f32*) &it_803F6A28 + ip->xDD4_itemVar.linkarrow.x9C with temp_r3[31] and temp_r3[23], to match original data-relative loads.
Control assert metadata by temporarily redefining __FILE__ to jobj.h around fake HSD_JObjSetRotation/Translate helpers.
Preserve compiler-sensitive control flow, including a goto end in itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield, when structured returns do not match.
Remove unnecessary casts and use direct typed gobj calls where this improves codegen and readability.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only comment was an automated decomp.dev report showing matched code increased by 5220 bytes and matched data by 256 bytes.

decomp.dev reported 9 new matches, including itlinkarrow .data, itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield, itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Coll, itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Coll, fn_802A81C4, three Yoshi SpecialS collision routines, and itLinkArrow_Logic98_Reflected.
decomp.dev also reported 5 improvements in still-unmatched items: itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Anim, it_802A850C, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Coll, and ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove or replace perm_vel_inline/perm_neg_inline/perm_mul_inline in ftYs_SpecialS.c.
Check whether lbcoll matching later allowed removal of fake_HSD_JObjSetRotation/fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate and the __FILE__ jobj.h workaround.
Look for later renames or struct typing of it_803F6A78 and it_803F6A98 once Link Arrow data is better understood.
Search subsequent itlinkarrow.c work for cleanup of fn_802A81C4 duplication in itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Anim.
Compare later decomp.dev reports for it_802A850C and itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim, which were improved but not fully matched in this PR.
